content.full_text:
SPORTFISHING ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA

2917 CANON STREET
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92106
(619) 226-6455

ROBERT C. FLETCHER                                                W. A. NOTT
PRESIDENT                                                         PRESIDENT-EMERITUS

November 7, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir:

The Sportfishing Association of California (SAC) is
requesting a formal written opinion on whether we should be
legally required to File Form 410 and Form 450. After
telephone discussions with staff of the Political Reform
Division of the Secretary of States' Office, it became clear
that there was confusion regarding my organization's need to
file the Statement of Organization, Form 410.

SAC was formed in 1972 to further the interests of the
Southern California Sportfishing Industry. The operating
budget comes from dues paid by the members. The last several
years the total operating budget has been approx. $150,000.00
annually. The members' dues are in no way earmarked for any
dedicated purpose, such as political donations, but are made
available for such purposes as the Board of Directors choose,
including a small percentage for campaign contributions. SAC
has one full time employee, myself, and a part-time clerical.
I am President and Treasurer, and spend my time mainly
working with state, federal and foreign administrators on
issues of interest or concern to the Sportfishing Fleet.

Since I became president and treasurer in March of 1989, SAC
has never donated more than aprox. $7,000.00 in any calendar
year. In addition, with the exception of the Pete Wilson
campaign to which SAC contributed $2,000.00 in calendar year
1990 after the court decision invalidated limits on
contributions, SAC has not donated in excess of $1,000.00 to
any candidate.

In light of the above information, I sincerely question
whether the Sportfishing Association of California qualifies
as a 'Recipient Committee' for the purposes of Form 410.
It appears to me that our filing of Form 635 regularly keeps
the state advised as to our political donations.

For purposes of background, I did file a form 410 recently
and then was notified that I had incurred a liability for
failure to notify The Secretary of State's Office within ten
days of qualification. My first question was how did I know
when SAC had qualified when the members' annual dues were in
no way designated? I had originally filed the Form 410 on
advice of the Political Reform Division staff. When I
received my identification number (902964) and the notice of
liability, I discussed all this with staff person Barbara
Smith, who suggested that I request the formal opinion.

Thank you for your time and consideration of our request, and
I can be reached at (619) 226-6455 during normal business
hours most week days if you require any additional
information.


Sincerely,

[signature]

Robert C. Fletcher, President/Treasurer
